public_reputation: Known around Soreheim Proper through day-to-day work as market clerk.
appearance: Theron Hearthwrit usually presents in layered civic fabric cut to show rank without inviting touch, with details that make their market clerk status legible before introductions begin.
manner: Thinks in queues, seals, and exchange rhythm, with patience only for those who can stay clear and orderly. In Soreheim Proper, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of market clerk in and around Soreheim Proper, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Soreheim Proper workable through the obligations of market clerk while managing the immediate pressure of quota demands, political rivalry, foreign pressure.
hidden_pressure: Must keep the role of market clerk credible while absorbing the strain created by quota demands, political rivalry, foreign pressure.

adventure_hooks.summary: Theron Hearthwrit can draw the party into a locality problem centered on market clerk duties in Soreheim Proper.
Theron Hearthwrit becomes useful when the party needs grounded help with quota demands, political rivalry, foreign pressure around Soreheim Proper.

support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe. V33_1 additive depth pass preserved canon while strengthening at-table identity, appearance, and social motive.
public_signal: In public, Theron Hearthwrit is immediately readable as a market clerk tied to Soreheim Proper.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Soreheim Proper: ordinary workspaces, public counters, roads, docks, shrines, workshops, or other role-relevant spaces
who_seeks_them_out: people who need access or clarity

first_impression: Theron Hearthwrit presents as someone shaped by the duties of a market clerk and the daily pressures of Soreheim Proper.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a market clerk, and proof that strangers will not make Soreheim Proper harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Theron Hearthwrit refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a market clerk too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Can explain permits, trade flow, and where ordinary disruptions have started to show.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Theron Hearthwrit adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Theron Hearthwrit always knows more about Soreheim Proper than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a market clerk in Soreheim Proper.
